title: QUIC CRT server client-auth transcript/runtime prerequisite architecture
purpose: Describe the smallest honest public server-auth gate on the existing SslServerAuthenticationOptions carrier, reusing the internal managed transcript/runtime prerequisite to emit TLS 1.3 CertificateRequest and accept client Certificate / CertificateVerify / Finished through the existing bridge without widening broader client-auth behavior.
risks: If the validator allows ClientCertificateRequired without the callback seam, the public gate would overclaim a client-auth floor the runtime cannot exercise.
If the client-certificate callback is modeled as a trust-store replacement, the slice will overclaim PKI behavior that was explicitly deferred.
If the public gate is left closed after the internal floor exists, the supported carrier will remain artificially blocked.
design_summary: The narrowest honest step is to open the existing public server-auth carrier only where the already-landed internal TLS transcript and bridge machinery can prove the behavior. The transcript owner now has a role-aware branch for an inbound client Certificate / CertificateVerify / Finished sequence after ClientHello when server client-auth is enabled, the key schedule emits one local CertificateRequest, and the transport bridge owns the existing callback seam that can accept or reject the presented client certificate without introducing a broader PKI policy surface. Broader chain/revocation customization, broader PKI behavior, and any public API widening beyond the existing carrier remain deferred.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This architecture slice now covers the public server-auth gate plus the internal transcript/runtime prerequisite: CertificateRequest emission, client Certificate / CertificateVerify / Finished acceptance, and callback-driven acceptance on the existing bridge seam. Broader chain/revocation customization and broader PKI behavior remain out of scope.
